harmfulSubstances.name: Tartrazine
harmfulSubstances.risk_summary: Hyperactivity in children, asthma, and potential carcinogenic links.
harmfulSubstances.plain_english_explainer: A bright yellow synthetic dye. Banned in Norway and Austria due to its links to hyperactivity in kids and allergic reactions like asthma.

harmfulSubstances.name: Sunset Yellow FCF
harmfulSubstances.risk_summary: Linked to hyperactivity, allergies, and thyroid tumors in animal studies.
harmfulSubstances.plain_english_explainer: An orange-red dye that can trigger hyperactivity in kids and worsen asthma. Restructured or banned in Scandinavia.

harmfulSubstances.name: Carmoisine
harmfulSubstances.risk_summary: Hyperactivity in children, allergen, banned in multiple countries.
harmfulSubstances.plain_english_explainer: A red food color. Strictly banned in the US and Japan because of hyperactivity concerns and potential allergic flare-ups.

harmfulSubstances.name: Amaranth
harmfulSubstances.risk_summary: Linked to cancer and birth defects; banned for food in the USA since 1976.
harmfulSubstances.plain_english_explainer: A dark red dye banned in the US for decades after animal studies linked it to malignant tumors.

harmfulSubstances.name: Ponceau 4R
harmfulSubstances.risk_summary: Hyperactivity, potential carcinogen, and kidney damage risk.
harmfulSubstances.plain_english_explainer: A strawberry red coal-tar dye. Banned in the US due to concerns over cancer and behavioral issues in kids.

harmfulSubstances.name: Erythrosine
harmfulSubstances.risk_summary: Thyroid disruption, chromosomal damage, and carcinogen in high doses.
harmfulSubstances.plain_english_explainer: A cherry-pink color that contains iodine. Banned in Europe for general food items as it interferes with thyroid function and can cause DNA damage.

harmfulSubstances.name: TBHQ
harmfulSubstances.risk_summary: Carcinogenic at high doses, immune disruption, DNA damage, and vision disturbance.
harmfulSubstances.plain_english_explainer: Tertiary Butylhydroquinone is a synthetic antioxidant used to prevent rancidity in fats. It is banned in Japan because long-term studies linked high doses to stomach tumors.

harmfulSubstances.name: BHA
harmfulSubstances.risk_summary: IARC Group 2B probable carcinogen, endocrine disruptor.
harmfulSubstances.plain_english_explainer: Butylated Hydroxyanisole is a waxy chemical used to preserve fats. The WHO classifies it as a suspected carcinogen and hormone disruptor.

harmfulSubstances.name: BHT
harmfulSubstances.risk_summary: Endocrine disruptor, lung tumor risk in animals, skin sensitizer.
harmfulSubstances.plain_english_explainer: Butylated Hydroxytoluene is similar to BHA. It can mimic estrogen, disrupting hormones, and is restricted in many European countries.

harmfulSubstances.name: Sodium Benzoate
harmfulSubstances.risk_summary: Forms benzene (a class-1 carcinogen) when mixed with Vitamin C (Ascorbic Acid).
banned_countries: Restricted in EU for children's beverages
harmfulSubstances.plain_english_explainer: A common preservative in soft drinks. If consumed alongside Vitamin C, they react to create Benzene, which damages DNA and can cause leukemia.

harmfulSubstances.name: Potassium Bromate
harmfulSubstances.risk_summary: Carcinogenic, damages kidneys, disrupts thyroid, and causes genetic mutations.
harmfulSubstances.status_india: banned (partially, but still found in bakery products)
harmfulSubstances.plain_english_explainer: Used to strengthen bread dough. It is a powerful carcinogen banned in almost all major economies. It remains a major concern in some Indian local bakery products.
harmfulSubstances.safer_alternative: Ascorbic Acid (Vitamin C) or Enzyme treatment

harmfulSubstances.name: Aspartame
harmfulSubstances.risk_summary: Classified as possibly carcinogenic (Group 2B) by IARC in 2023. Triggers headaches and mood issues in some.
harmfulSubstances.plain_english_explainer: A highly popular artificial sugar substitute. It was declared a possible carcinogen in 2023. Highly dangerous for people with phenylketonuria (PKU).

harmfulSubstances.name: Acesulfame-K
harmfulSubstances.risk_summary: Thyroid disruption in animal studies; contains methylene chloride (a known solvent).
harmfulSubstances.plain_english_explainer: An artificial sweetener often blended with Aspartame. Some studies suggest it can harm gut health and alter thyroid function.

harmfulSubstances.name: Cyclamate
harmfulSubstances.risk_summary: Linked to bladder cancer and testicular atrophy in animal tests.
harmfulSubstances.plain_english_explainer: An early artificial sweetener. Banned in the United States and UK since 1969 due to high bladder tumor risk in lab mice.

encyclopediaIngredients.name: Maida
encyclopediaIngredients.what_is_it: Wheat flour that has been stripped of its fiber, bran, and key nutrients, then bleached.
encyclopediaIngredients.what_it_does_to_body: Causes rapid spikes in blood glucose due to high glycemic index, leading to insulin resistance, fat storage, and increased diabetes risk.

encyclopediaIngredients.name: Palm Oil
encyclopediaIngredients.what_is_it: An edible vegetable oil derived from the fruit of oil palms.
encyclopediaIngredients.what_it_does_to_body: Highly saturated fat content increases LDL (bad) cholesterol, raising the risk of cardiovascular disease. High in inflammatory omega-6 fatty acids.

encyclopediaIngredients.name: Monosodium Glutamate
encyclopediaIngredients.what_is_it: The sodium salt of glutamic acid, used to add 'umami' savory flavor.
encyclopediaIngredients.what_it_does_to_body: Can cause flushing, headaches, sweating, and rapid heartbeat in sensitive individuals (MSG symptom complex). Highly addictive, encouraging overeating.

encyclopediaIngredients.name: High Fructose Corn Syrup
encyclopediaIngredients.what_is_it: A liquid sweetener made from corn starch, high in free fructose.
encyclopediaIngredients.what_it_does_to_body: Processed directly by the liver, converting into fat and causing fatty liver disease (NAFLD), obesity, and type-2 diabetes.

encyclopediaIngredients.name: Carrageenan
encyclopediaIngredients.what_is_it: A gum extracted from red seaweed, used to thicken foods.
encyclopediaIngredients.what_it_does_to_body: Can cause gastrointestinal inflammation, bloating, and IBS flare-ups. Animal studies link degraded carrageenan to colon ulcers.

alternatives.reason: Slurrp Farm Millet Noodles contain 100% whole grain Ragi and Foxtail Millet, air-dried (not fried in palm oil), and have 60% less sodium.
Nestle Oats Noodles are a fiber-rich alternative with whole grain oats flour and reduced maida.

seedProducts.name: Kurkure Masala Munch
seedProducts.manufacturer: PepsiCo India Holdings Pvt. Ltd., Gurgaon

alternatives.reason: The Better Munch Baked Ragi Puffs are baked, not fried, contain zero palm oil, and have a 100% clean whole grain ragi base.
